## Report

**Files changed (both under `apps/rt`, my lane):**
- `C:\Atiz\mustard\apps\rt\src\commands\spec\approve_spec.rs` — refactored the gate section of `run()`.
- `C:\Atiz\mustard\apps\rt\src\hooks\observe\approval_marker_observer.rs` — hardened `active_spec`.

**What changed:**
1. `approve_spec.rs` — the clarify (F6, `.clarified`) and approval (T5, `.approved-by-user`) markers are now evaluated **together** instead of exiting on the first miss. Replaced the two didactic constants + the two `enforce_approval_gate` calls with one aggregated builder `unmet_gate_message(spec, clarify_missing, approval_missing) -> Option<String>` and a single `enforce_gate(approval_gate(mode, false), &msg)`. The refusal lists every missing marker with its minting path (clarify: `grill-capture --finalize --spec <spec>`; approval: ExitPlanMode / approval AskUserQuestion). Exit code 1 and strict|warn|off semantics are preserved — `approval_gate` (the mode decision) is reused unchanged; warn prints the identical text to stderr; off short-circuits before the gate.
2. `approval_marker_observer.rs` — added a third fallback to `active_spec` (shared with `plan_approval_observer`): `unique_pending_full_plan(cwd)`. When neither the session binding nor the `.pipeline-states/` hint resolves, it returns the **unique** spec that is `scope=full` + `stage=Plan` + not-yet-approved; zero or 2+ candidates return `None` (fail-closed). It reuses the observer's own `is_full_plan` + `already_approved` predicates so it can't drift from the gate's fact-1 window.

**Non-obvious decisions:**
- Reused `is_full_plan`/`already_approved` (fact-1 predicates) for the fallback rather than `active_specs.rs`'s broader `classify_spec` (which treats Analyze/Plan/Execute all as Active) — that classifier would widen the window past the pending-approval state.
- Observer end-to-end mint assertions are guarded by `MUSTARD_ACTIVE_SPEC` being unset (since `active_spec` still consults `current_spec` first) so they never flake on an ambient env; the deterministic resolver assertions carry the real guarantee.

**Tests added (all passing):** `approve_spec` — combined_refusal_lists_all_missing_gates, clarify_only_missing_refuses_with_single_requirement, approval_only_missing_refuses_with_single_requirement, both_present_approves. `approval_marker_observer` — unique_pending_full_plan_resolves_without_binding, two_pending_full_plans_stay_none.

**Verification:** `cargo check -p mustard-rt` clean; `approve_spec::tests` 17 passed (13+4), `approval_marker_observer::tests` 12 passed (10+2), `plan_approval_observer::tests` 7 passed (unchanged — shared `active_spec` change safe); clippy 0 errors, 0 warnings in touched files.

**Blocker note (resolved):** early build attempts failed to compile the lib test binary due to stale test code in `apps/rt/src/commands/wave/wave_scaffold.rs` (`traceability_gaps` arity / `TraceGaps` shape) — a concurrent wave's dirty file (` M`), not mine. I did not touch it (write-write collision risk); it was fixed by that wave shortly after, and all my tests then compiled and passed.

<MEMORY>approval_marker_observer's unbound fallback resolves the UNIQUE full/Plan/unapproved spec and returns None on 2+ candidates — NOT newest-by-mtime like the current_spec/session_id fallbacks — because mis-attributing a real user approval to the wrong Full spec forges approval, which is worse than minting nothing.</MEMORY></result>

## Report

**Files changed (all under `apps/rt/`):**

- **`src/commands/agent/render/role.rs`** — Added `pub const PLUGIN_NAMESPACE: &str = "mustard"` and a private `qualify_plugin_agent(name) -> String` helper. Changed `recommended_subagent_type` to return `String`, qualifying review/qa→`mustard:mustard-review`, guards→`mustard:mustard-guards`, patterns→`mustard:mustard-patterns`; builtins (`Explore`/`Plan`/`general-purpose`) stay bare. Updated the existing resolver test, added `recommended_subagent_type_namespaces_plugin_agents_only` and the drift guard `plugin_namespace_matches_manifest_name` (reads `plugin/.claude-plugin/plugin.json` via `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`, fail-open skip if absent, asserts `name == PLUGIN_NAMESPACE`).
- **`src/hooks/task/subagent_inject.rs`** — `role_is_readonly` now strips a `<ns>:` prefix (`split_once(':')`) before matching and adds `mustard-patterns` to the read-only set. Added test `role_is_readonly_normalises_namespaced_plugin_agents`.
- **`src/commands/pipeline/dispatch_plan.rs`** — **FINDING #7:** extended `wave_number_from_link` with a `wave.` dotted branch (`strip_prefix("wave.")` → split on `.` → new `leading_wave_number` helper reads the LAST `<N>-<role>` segment, falling back to a numeric head segment); hyphen and bare-role branches untouched. Updated struct doc + both call sites (dropped now-redundant `.to_string()`). Added test `dotted_wikilink_resolves_wave`.
- **`src/commands/pipeline/wave_advance.rs`** — Updated module/`review_round` doc comments, one call site, and two test asserts from `mustard-review` to `mustard:mustard-review`.

**Non-obvious decisions:**
- Chose **last-segment-first** parsing for the dotted form so a slug with leading digits in a middle segment (`wave.2024-redesign.3-agents`) resolves to 3, not 2024; `leading_wave_number` requires the digits to be followed by `-` or end-of-segment so an alphanumeric slug like `2fa-login` can't masquerade as a wave number.
- The drift guard fails **open** (skips with an eprintln when the manifest can't be resolved from `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR`), matching this crate's established convention for tests reaching outside the crate (`gate_regression_check`, `spec_invariants`).

**Verification:** No snapshots or callers outside `apps/rt` reference these agent strings, so the `&'static str`→`String` signature change and the value change (`mustard-review`→`mustard:mustard-review`) ripple nowhere else. Boundary-gate WARNs on every edit were expected (role.rs/subagent_inject.rs aren't in the spec's `## Files`) — I did not touch the spec.

<MEMORY>The orchestrator must pass plugin-owned subagent_types namespaced as `mustard:mustard-*` (Claude Code registers plugin agents under the plugin.json `name`); a bare `mustard-review` silently falls back to general-purpose. Keep PLUGIN_NAMESPACE as the single source (drift-tested against the manifest) — this forces recommended_subagent_type to return an owned String, since a const can't be concat!'d into a &'static str.</MEMORY></result>

## Report

Implemented the `Expect:` evidence-regex contract for QA acceptance criteria. All 6 tasks complete; build clean (1 attempt), clippy clean on edited files, full `mustard-rt` suite 3070 passed.

Files changed (all under apps/rt):
- `apps/rt/Cargo.toml` — added `regex = "1"` inline (mirrors apps/scan; already resolved in Cargo.lock via scan, so no new version).
- `apps/rt/src/commands/review/qa_run/mod.rs` — `AcItem.expect: Option<String>`; factored `extract_command`'s body into a shared `extract_marker` spine + new `extract_expect`; lookahead now captures a same-line or following-line `Expect:`; `run_qa`'s items tuple carries `expect` through (`(id, command, expect)`), capability ACs map to `None`.
- `apps/rt/src/commands/review/qa_run/runner.rs` — pure `ExpectVerdict` enum + `evaluate_expect` (SRP matcher, fail-open on bad regex) + `excerpt` helper; `run_ac_command` gained `expect: Option<&str>`; on exit-0 it now gates on the regex; 4 existing call sites updated to `None`; 5 new `expect_regex_*` tests.
- `apps/rt/src/commands/review/analyze_validation.rs` — `is_test_shaped_command` predicate + V6b lint (`test-ac-no-expect`, WARN) + 1 `expect_regex` test.

Non-obvious decisions:
- On an Expect miss the result is `status: "fail"` but `exit: Some(0)` — honest (the process did exit 0; the fail is the evidence gate), with an excerpt naming the pattern and the output.
- Matching uses the full combined output; the pre-existing 100-char truncation is display-only (see MEMORY).
- `test-ac-no-expect` deliberately excludes ids already flagged `weak-ac` (a tautology's fix is replacement, not adding an Expect line), plus the trailing safety AC and `<…>` skeletons — mirroring V6. Effect: it fires for strong-but-unasserted test commands (e.g. a filtered `cargo test foo` with no Expect).
- No-Expect path is byte-for-byte the legacy verdict (empty excerpt on pass; identical excerpt on real fail) — confirmed by the unchanged snapshot/integration tests.

No blockers. Boundary gate emitted WARNs (all edits are inside apps/rt, which the ROLE and task scope explicitly authorize); mode was `warn`, nothing blocked.</result>

<result>All wiring is coherent and verified. Let me confirm the build-attempt budget and give a final summary of the verification state.

Implementation complete and green. Summary below.

## What changed

**`apps/rt/src/commands/wave/wave_scaffold.rs`** (core)
- `traceability_gaps(plan, parent_ac_md: Option<&str>)` now returns a `TraceGaps { untraced_waves, uncovered_acs }` struct instead of a flat `Vec<String>`, splitting Gap 1 (always-WARN) from Gap 2 (escalatable). It extends the `defined` id set with the parent `spec.md` `## Acceptance Criteria` ids read through the shared `qa_run::extract_ac_section` + `parse_ac_items` (no forked parser).
- `scaffold` reads the parent monolithic spec (`spec.md`, falling back to `spec.original.md`), resolves `MUSTARD_TRACE_GATE_MODE` via the shared `resolve_mode`/`GateMode` (default `strict`), always prints Gap 1 as WARN, prints Gap 2 as WARN in warn/strict (silent in off), and threads the block back typed.
- New pure helper `trace_block_for(mode, uncovered_acs)` — strict carries the uncovered ACs through, warn/off return empty. Extracted so the escalation is unit-testable without mutating the process env (`set_var` is `unsafe` in edition 2024 and the crate avoids it).
- `ScaffoldOutcome::Created` gained a `trace_block: Vec<String>` field; `run` emits `error` + a deterministic (BTreeSet-sorted) `trace_gaps` array and `exit(2)` when it is non-empty, else the byte-stable success JSON is unchanged.
- Added 4 `parent_spec_ac` unit tests + updated the existing traceability test to the new shape.

**`apps/rt/src/commands/pipeline/plan_materialize.rs`** — destructures `trace_block: _` and deliberately ignores it (the gate is on the `wave-scaffold` command, not the in-process pipeline composite).

**`apps/rt/tests/wave_scaffold_contract.rs`** — 2 subprocess integration tests proving the real exit contract: strict → exit 2 with `trace_gaps` listing the uncovered AC (layout still materialised); `MUSTARD_TRACE_GATE_MODE=warn` → exit 0 with the gap only on stderr.

## Non-obvious decisions
- The escalation lives in the `run` face (via the typed `trace_block`), never inside `scaffold` — mirroring the existing EmptyPlan/Unreadable pattern so `scaffold` stays reusable in-process. `plan-materialize` therefore does not block.
- Default-strict does not break existing tests because none seed a parent `spec.md` with uncovered ACs (absent parent → empty `defined`), so `uncovered_acs` stays empty and nothing blocks.

## Verification
- `cargo check` / clippy `--all-targets`: clean, exit 0 — no diagnostics on either changed file (listed clippy warnings are pre-existing debt in untouched files).
- Tests: full `mustard-rt` suite 3070 passed; `wave_scaffold` module 22 passed; `wave_scaffold_contract` 8 passed. No blockers; 1 build cycle used.</result>
